returned.checked_work: review Brick for task-source:inline-statement '# Gemini API HTTP 400 repair design' — a read-only diagnosis/design plan node whose declared return shape is the plan return template (objective_summary, scope_boundaries, constraints, missing_scope, proposed_next_work_boundaries, observed_evidence, not_proven, transition_concern_evidence).
missing_or_mismatched_facts: Line-number drift in several citations: the plan cites adapter_constants.py:48/49 (actual 54/55), agent_adapter.py:588/589/580 (actual 709/710/701), adapter_local_cli.py:362/383 (actual 745/766), agent_axis_behavioral.yaml:448-449 (actual 473-474), and kernel_checks.py gemini literals at :3242/:3318-3319/:3350/:3360 (the actual run_gemini_api_adapter spans 3389-3604; expected_url 3517-3520, api_model_name 3550, body 3531-3543; the cited 3242/3317-3360 region is the codex_connect_stall checker, not the gemini checker). Symbol names, constant values, and described behaviors all match current sources; only the line anchors are stale. adapter_gemini_http.py line citations (87-97, 108-112, 115, 176-179, 267) are accurate.

Checker model-literal count: the plan describes 'three model literals (expected_url, api_model_name, build-request)' to update in lockstep, but run_gemini_api_adapter contains two model-name literals (expected_url line 3519, api_model_name line 3550); the body-shape assertion (3531-3543) carries no model name. The cited :3360 '_build_gemini_api_request literal' falls outside the gemini checker. This does not break the repair scope (targets are reachable by symbol) but the literal count/anchor is imprecise.

adapter-error.json source artifact itself was not re-opened in this review; its contents are taken from the plan's transcription and the task-source restatement (both agree on adapter:gemini-api / model:gemini:default / ValueError / 'HTTP error status 400').
narrowly_proven: The plan node's returned object structurally satisfies the declared plan return shape (all 8 required fields present, no forbidden keys).

Every substantive code/checker claim I re-derived from current sources is accurate at the symbol/value/behavior level; the gemini-api request envelope is doc-aligned except the model id, and the model literal is duplicated across adapter constant, fallback, casting fallbacks, and checker.
The plan's five required analyses are each present and grounded in named local surfaces.
not_proven: Whether HTTP 400 root cause is the model-id drift vs another INVALID_ARGUMENT cause — body discarded by design; not locally observable without a live diagnostic call (out of scope, an environment/runtime constraint, not a code defect).
Whether 'gemini-3.5-flash' is a currently-served v1beta model id — relies on COO-supplied doc evidence, not verified live here.
Live four-LLM lane behavior, credential/key/project/location validity, and provider response after the repair.

adapter_local_cli.py lines 745/766 and adapter-error.json contents were partially cross-checked but not the full surrounding control flow; checker --all EXIT 0 was not executed (read-only review, no command run).
source truth, success judgment, quality judgment, Movement authority — none asserted; sufficiency and Movement belong to the Link gate, quality/approval to the human.

No transition_concern_evidence is raised: the cited inaccuracies are stale line anchors and an over-count of one checker literal, not a reproduced defect that blocks the downstream WRITE Building (targets remain reachable by symbol); the 400 itself originates in a different building with no in-building upstream WORK node to reroute to.
observed_evidence: Prior plan step-output read at work/step-outputs/gemini-api-http400-repair-design-0625-plan-attempt-1/step-output.json: returned object carries objective_summary, scope_boundaries, constraints, missing_scope, proposed_next_work_boundaries, observed_evidence, not_proven, transition_concern_evidence (=null), plus proof_limits and CLI envelope fields.

support/connection/adapter_constants.py: MODEL_REF_GEMINI_FLASH='model:gemini:gemini-2.5-flash' (line 54), MODEL_REF_GEMINI_LOCAL_FLASH='model:gemini:gemini-3.5-flash' (line 55) — both exist as the plan asserts.

support/connection/agent_adapter.py: _GEMINI_API_BASE_URL='https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com/v1beta/models' (line 709), _GEMINI_API_MODEL_FALLBACK='gemini-2.5-flash' (line 710), gemini-api spec default_model_ref=MODEL_REF_GEMINI_FLASH (line 701), gemini-LOCAL spec default_model_ref=MODEL_REF_GEMINI_LOCAL_FLASH (line 683).

support/connection/adapter_gemini_http.py: _gemini_api_model_name falls back to _GEMINI_API_MODEL_FALLBACK (lines 87-97); url='{base}/{model_name}:generateContent' and body {'contents':[{'parts':[{'text':...}]}]} (lines 108-112); header x-goog-api-key (line 115); HTTPError collapsed to 'gemini-api HTTP error status {code}' with body discarded (lines 176-179); invoke_gemini_text default model_name='gemini-2.5-flash' (line 267).

support/connection/adapter_model_casting.py line 191: GEMINI_LOCAL short-circuit is gated on spec.adapter_ref==ADAPTER_GEMINI_LOCAL, so it does NOT apply to gemini-api; 'model:gemini:default' then returns '' via the model_id in {'default','inherit'} branch (lines 200-201).

support/checkers/lib/kernel_checks.py run_gemini_api_adapter (lines 3389-3604): expected_url embeds 'gemini-2.5-flash:generateContent' (lines 3517-3520), api_model_name literal 'gemini-2.5-flash' (line 3550), body-shape assertion contents/parts/text (lines 3531-3543), no-key->local_cli_missing with no subprocess (lines 3457-3481).

support/checkers/profiles/agent_axis_behavioral.yaml: gemini_api_adapter registered (line 22); gemini-LOCAL casting row pins selected_model_ref model:gemini:gemini-3.5-flash / expected_cli_arg gemini-3.5-flash (lines 473-474).
support/connection/adapter_local_cli.py: gemini fallback 'gemini-2.5-flash' (line 745) vs 'gemini-3.5-flash' (line 766) — the intra-file 2.5/3.5 inconsistency the plan flags exists.
observed_matches: Return-shape completeness: all 8 plan-return-template required fields are present in the returned object; transition_concern_evidence is explicitly null with a recorded rationale; no forbidden keys (success/failure/approved/quality/movement_choice/route_target) appear.

All 5 required analyses from the task source are addressed: (1) strongest LOCAL root-cause candidates (gemini-api model-ref drift to 2.5-flash + body-discarding HTTPError classification); (2) decision-2 disposition proposes BOTH (model align + safe status-category recording) as a caller-decided proposal; (3) candidate_write_scope_files + checker_plan name exact files/checkers; (4) verification_plan covers mocked checker, no-key path, URL/header/body capture, and a live four-LLM lane expectation that accepts a clean adapter-error; (5) proof_limits + not_proven are populated.

Substantive code claims verified against current sources: gemini-api fallback model is 'gemini-2.5-flash'; request envelope (base URL v1beta/models, x-goog-api-key header, contents/parts/text body) matches the COO-supplied doc evidence except the model id; the 400 body is deliberately discarded; the GEMINI_LOCAL casting short-circuit does not cover gemini-api; MODEL_REF_GEMINI_LOCAL_FLASH (gemini-3.5-flash) already exists; the gemini_api_adapter checker locks expected_url + api_model_name + body shape and is registered in agent_axis_behavioral.yaml.

The checker-lockstep constraint the plan raises is real: changing the gemini-api model literal requires updating the checker's expected_url (kernel_checks.py:3519) and api_model_name (kernel_checks.py:3550) to keep --all green.
Axis discipline observed in the returned object: read-only posture stated, no Movement/route/quality/success keys, scope explicitly bounds the actual repair to a separate downstream WRITE Building.
